instruction: Monitor Ubuntu system resource usage using the sar command from sysstat toolkit. Collect CPU statistics every second for 30 seconds and save the output to 'System_Resources_Report.txt' on Desktop.
config: _command_setup(command='echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S apt-get update && echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S apt-get install sysstat', shell='true')

evaluator.eval: file_contains(get_vm_file(env, config={'path': '/home/user/Desktop/System_Resources_Report.txt', 'dest': 'System_Resources_Report.txt'}), get_rule(env, config={'rules': {'expected': ['CPU', '%user', '%nice', '%system', '%iowait', '%steal', '%idle']}})) and check_line_number(get_vm_file(env, config={'path': '/home/user/Desktop/System_Resources_Report.txt', 'dest': 'System_Resources_Report.txt'}), get_rule(env, config={'rules': {'expected': '31'}}))
